_generated: AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/gen_catalog.py -- DO NOT EDIT; regenerate with `python3 scripts/gen_catalog.py`.  Single-source, machine-readable map of the Alp SDK surface, projected from metadata/, examples/, include/alp/, and scripts/.  Presence/structure only (no driver tier or GA/stub status). A CI gate keeps it byte-in-sync with the sources.

emit_modes.description: Per-core build plan (board target, app dir, toolchain) the orchestrator fans out.
Devicetree flash/storage partition nodes for the project's memory layout.
Devicetree /reserved-memory nodes for the cross-core shared-memory carve-outs.
C header defining the inter-core IPC contract (shared-memory + mailbox channel layout).
C source registering the project's storage mount points.
Per-project system manifest (cores, slices, memory, helper firmware) the build + flash flow consumes.
TF-M sysbuild Kconfig fragment for the secure-processing-environment build.

aen.name: aen-analog-validate
aen.summary: On-silicon analog validation for the **E1M-AEN801** (Alif Ensemble E8, M55-HE), via the bench RAM-run + RAM-console flow.

aen.name: aen-cc3501e-bringup
aen.summary: Host (Alif) side of the on-module **TI CC3501E** Wi-Fi 6 + BLE 5.4 coprocessor bring-up on the **E1M-AEN801** (Alif Ensemble E8) SoM.

aen.name: aen-cc3501e-companion-tour
aen.summary: The **capstone** full-surface example for the on-module **TI CC3501E** Wi-Fi 6 + BLE 5.4 coprocessor, driven from the Alif **M55-HE** core on the **E1M-AEN801** (Alif Ensemble E8) SoM.

aen.name: aen-cc3501e-gpio
aen.summary: Host (Alif) side of the on-module **TI CC3501E** GPIO proxy + camera-enable demo on the **E1M-AEN801** (Alif Ensemble E8) SoM.

aen.name: aen-eeprom-manifest
aen.summary: Read + decode the 128-byte Alp hardware-info manifest from the on-module **24C128 EEPROM** on the E1M-AEN (Alif Ensemble) SoM, over the portable `<alp/*>` API. The AEN sibling of [`v2n-eeprom-manifest-dump`](../../v2n/v2n-eeprom-manifest-dump) (same `src/` flow).

aen.name: aen-secure-element-sign
aen.summary: Probe the OPTIGA Trust M on the E1M-AEN's **BRD_I2C** and confirm the current probe-only driver contract. The driver reads I2C_STATE to prove the part is reachable; product-info and raw-APDU helpers return `ALP_ERR_NOSUPPORT` until the Infineon host-library transport is integrated.

aen.name: edgeai-vision-aen
aen.summary: End-to-end EdgeAI reference application for the **E1M EVK** populated with an **E1M-AEN801** SoM (Alif Ensemble E8 -- the lead AEN part).

ai.name: ai-anomaly-detection-vibration
ai.summary: > ⚠️ **`[UNTESTED]` -- v0.5 paper-correct.** Compiles on > `native_sim/native/64` + cross-compiles to > `ensemble_e8_dk/ae402fa0e5597le0/rtss_hp`. HiL bring-up with a > real motor / pump and a customer-trained model is v0.6 work.

ai.name: cold-chain-monitor
ai.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** The `cold_chain` core > is host-unit-tested on `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs end-to-end on > native_sim with synthetic environment data covering the states. HiL with a > real BME280 + a trained model is bench-gated.

ai.name: motor-current-signature
ai.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** The `current_features` > core is host-unit-tested on `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs > end-to-end on native_sim with synthetic current covering all five states. HiL > on a real motor + a trained model is bench-gated.

ai.name: multimodal-fusion-pdm
ai.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** The `fusion_health` > core is host-unit-tested on `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs > end-to-end on native_sim with synthetic per-modality data covering every > scenario. HiL with the three real sensors + a trained model is bench-gated.

ai.name: rail-predictive-maintenance
ai.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.6 paper-correct.** The DSP cores > (`rail_features`, `rail_position`) are host-unit-tested on > `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs end-to-end on native_sim with > a canned NMEA track + synthetic vibration. HiL on a real bogie with a > customer-trained model is bench-gated.

ai.name: visual-defect-detection
ai.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** The `defect_map` core > is host-unit-tested on `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs end-to-end on > native_sim with synthetic clean + defect frames. HiL with a real CSI camera + > a trained autoencoder is bench-gated.

ai.name: wearable-activity-fall
ai.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** The two cores > (`motion_features`, `fall_detect`) are host-unit-tested on > `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs end-to-end on native_sim with > synthetic motion (incl. an injected fall). HiL on a real wearable + a trained > model is bench-gated.

audio.name: acoustic-anomaly-wind-turbine
audio.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.6 paper-correct.** The three DSP cores > (`acoustic_features`, `rotor_speed`, `bpf_modulation`) are host-unit-tested on > `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs end-to-end on native_sim with > synthetic acoustics + a canned RPM track. HiL on a real nacelle with a > customer-trained model is bench-gated.

audio.name: acoustic-safety-events
audio.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** The `acoustic_event` > core is host-unit-tested on `native_sim/native/64`; the full app runs > end-to-end on native_sim with synthetic audio cycling the four event types. > HiL with a real mic + a trained model is bench-gated.

audio.name: audio-loopback
audio.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/audio.h>`. Reads PCM blocks from the on-module PDM microphone, runs them through the ALP DSP chain (DC-block in v0.2), and pushes them straight back out over I²S. Demonstrates the full `audio_in` / `audio_out` lifecycle.

audio.name: audio-noise-suppression
audio.summary: Real-time noise suppression demo on the V2N (and V2H by SKU flip). Target market: USB-C headsets, true-wireless hearables, desktop conferencing rigs -- anywhere you want cloud-grade denoising at sub-perceptual latency without shipping audio off-device.

audio.name: audio-wake-word
audio.summary: > ![status: UNTESTED](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-%5BUNTESTED%5D-orange) > v0.5 paper-correct. Builds clean on `native_sim/native/64`; real > PDM mic capture + Ethos-U55 dispatch + Vela-compiled model land > in v0.6 AEN HiL.

audio.name: i2s-tone
audio.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/i2s.h>`. Generates a 1 kHz sine wave and writes it as 16-bit stereo PCM to ALP_E1M_I2S0.

audio.name: libhelix-decode
audio.summary: Demonstrates the [Helix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helix_(codec)) fixed-point MP3 decoder workflow: MP3 frame synchronisation, header parsing, and (on ARM silicon) fixed-point PCM decode.

audio.name: minimp3-decode
audio.summary: Decodes a canned MP3 byte array to 16-bit PCM with the vendored [minimp3](https://github.com/lieff/minimp3) single-header decoder and prints the sample count + RMS of the decoded audio. Pure compute -- no I2S/codec hardware required, so this builds and runs identically on `native_sim` and real silicon.

camera-vision.name: ai-camera-viewer
camera-vision.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** Builds clean on > `native_sim/native/64` against the full `<alp/camera.h>` / > `<alp/inference.h>` surfaces (both ship today; Ethos-U dispatch > runs through `alp_inference_open(...)` same as any other backend). > What's still missing is a real Vela-compiled `person_detect.tflite` > model and AEN HiL bench validation.

camera-vision.name: ai-object-detection-realtime
camera-vision.summary: > **`[UNTESTED]` on hardware -- v0.9 paper-correct.** Builds clean on > `native_sim/native/64` + the AEN fallback target against the full > `<alp/camera.h>` / `<alp/inference.h>` / `<alp/display.h>` surfaces, > which all ship today -- DEEPX DX-M1 dispatch runs through > `alp_inference_open(...)` same as any other backend. What's still > missing is a real Vela-/DXNN-compiled YOLOv8-tiny model (see "Adding > the model" below) and V2N-M1 HiL bench validation.

connectivity.name: coap-client-get
connectivity.summary: `[UNTESTED]` -- native_sim build+run PASSED locally (see below), but this has not been verified on real silicon (no bench/HIL sweep).

connectivity.name: iot-connected-camera
connectivity.summary: Connected-camera reference application for the **E1M EVK** populated with an **E1M-V2N101 / E1M-V2N102** SoM (Renesas RZ/V2N).

connectivity.name: iot-dashboard
connectivity.summary: > ⚠️ **`[UNTESTED]` -- v0.5 paper-correct.** Builds clean on > `native_sim/native/64`; real WiFi + MQTT + TLS bring-up gates on > v0.6 AEN HiL.

connectivity.name: iot-fleet-ota
connectivity.summary: Secure OTA firmware update with rollback. The production-readiness proof for "how do we update 10 000 units in the field?".

connectivity.name: jsmn-json-parse
connectivity.summary: `[UNTESTED]` -- native_sim build+run PASSED locally (see below), but this has not been verified on real silicon (no bench/HIL sweep).

connectivity.name: modbus-server
connectivity.summary: Modbus teaching example for the ADR 0018 `libraries: [modbus]` manifest.

connectivity.name: mqtt-sn-publish
connectivity.summary: coreMQTT-SN teaching example. Encodes an MQTT-SN PUBLISH packet into a buffer, then parses it back out of the same buffer and asserts the topic id, message id, QoS, retain flag, and payload all survive the round trip. No socket, no gateway, no live transport -- this is the unit-test shape you'd run before wiring the encoder to a real UDP/ BLE/UART MQTT-SN link.

connectivity.name: nanopb-encode-decode
connectivity.summary: `[UNTESTED]` -- native_sim build+run PASSED locally (see below), but this has not been verified on real silicon (no bench/HIL sweep).

connectivity.name: production-deployment
connectivity.summary: The v1.0 reference application for a field-deployable IoT product. Demonstrates the full manufactured -> deployed -> updated -> attested lifecycle on a single app, so customers see how the SDK's secure-boot, OTA, EEPROM-provisioning, and remote-attestation pieces fit together.

connectivity.name: tinygsm-modem-at
connectivity.summary: Runs the cellular-modem AT bring-up flow [TinyGSM](https://github.com/vshymanskyy/TinyGSM) drives -- an `AT` liveness probe, then `ATI` / `AT+CGMI` / `AT+GMM` for `getModemInfo()` -- against a mock AT-transcript `Stream`, and prints the assembled modem info. No real UART or cellular modem required.

connectivity.name: websocket-frame
connectivity.summary: libwebsockets teaching example. Masks and encodes a single unfragmented RFC 6455 TEXT frame into a buffer, then decodes (unmasks) it back out and asserts the payload survives the round trip. No socket, no TLS, no handshake -- this is the frame-layout byte accounting you'd want to get right before ever opening a real connection.

display.name: drone-hud
display.summary: > ⚠️ **`[UNTESTED]` -- v0.5 paper-correct.** Builds on > `native_sim/native/64`; HiL silicon bring-up of the IMU + GPS + > battery + display chain lands in v0.6.

display.name: gfx-compat-blit
display.summary: **[UNTESTED]** -- `native_sim` build-and-run only; not yet run on a bench SoM.

display.name: lvgl-benchmark
display.summary: Wraps the upstream **`lv_demo_benchmark()`** -- the canonical LVGL performance harness. Walks a fixed scene set (gradients, alpha blends, text rendering, image rotation, ...) and reports per-scene FPS at the end.

display.name: lvgl-dashboard-x-evk
display.summary: A minimal LVGL 9 dashboard on the E1M-X V2N MIPI-DSI panel (RK055HDMIPI4MA0, 720 x 1280 @ 60 Hz).

display.name: lvgl-music-player
display.summary: Wraps the upstream **`lv_demo_music()`** -- album-art carousel, time slider, track list, equaliser visualiser. This is a **display-only** demo: it renders and animates the player UI but does not decode or play any audio.

display.name: lvgl-widgets-demo
display.summary: Wraps the upstream **`lv_demo_widgets()`** from LVGL's `demos/` tree -- the headline showcase of every standard LVGL widget (buttons, sliders, tabs, charts, meters, lists, animations).

display.name: u8g2-oled-draw
display.summary: **[UNTESTED]** -- `native_sim` build-and-run only; not yet run on a bench SoM.

multicore.name: heterogeneous-offload
multicore.summary: > `[UNTESTED]` -- v0.6 paper-correct. Builds against the same V2N > memory_map as rpmsg-v2n (no TBD addresses). HiL bring-up gates > on v0.8.

multicore.name: mproc-mailbox
multicore.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/mproc.h>`. Demonstrates the M55-HP side of a Cortex-M55-HP ↔ Cortex-M55-HE mailbox roundtrip on AEN: stage a payload in shared SRAM, signal the peer via the hardware mailbox, wait for a reply, read the result back.

multicore.name: rpmsg-aen
multicore.summary: > `[UNTESTED]` -- v0.6 structural draft. Board.yaml + sources are > shape-correct, but the end-to-end RPMsg path still needs AEN801 > bench validation.

multicore.name: rpmsg-imx93
multicore.summary: > `[UNTESTED]` -- v0.6 structural draft. Board.yaml + sources are > shape-correct; the build will fail at carve-out resolution until > the user supplies authoritative iMX93 memory-map values in > `metadata/e1m_modules/E1M-NX9101.yaml`. The metadata is TBD by > design (project memory note: don't invent HW values); the > declarative structural files are the contract that lets the > orchestrator land cleanly the moment those values are filled in.

multicore.name: rpmsg-v2n
multicore.summary: > `[UNTESTED]` -- v0.6 paper-correct. Both halves declared in one > `board.yaml` v2; the orchestrator fans out Yocto + Zephyr in > parallel. HiL bring-up on V2N + Yocto gates on v0.8.

peripheral-io.name: adc-voltmeter
peripheral-io.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/adc.h>`. Reads ADC channel `ALP_E1M_ADC1` once and prints the converted microvolt value. Demonstrates capability validation: requesting a resolution higher than the active SoC's `ALP_SOC_ADC_MAX_RESOLUTION_BITS` fails at `alp_adc_open` with `ALP_ERR_OUT_OF_RANGE`.

peripheral-io.name: alp-console
peripheral-io.summary: <!-- SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 -->

peripheral-io.name: can-loopback
peripheral-io.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/can.h>`. Brings up CAN0 in loopback mode, sends a frame, demonstrates the receive-callback contract.

peripheral-io.name: dac-waveform
peripheral-io.summary: Generate a sine wave on `BOARD_DAC0`.

peripheral-io.name: drone-autopilot
peripheral-io.summary: > ⚠️ **`[UNTESTED]` -- v0.5 paper-correct.** Compiles on > `native_sim/native/64` + cross-compiles to `ensemble_e8_dk/ae402fa0e5597le0/rtss_hp`. > **Do NOT fly this build.** The PID gains, sensor scales, > failsafe thresholds, and motor mapping are starting-point > values that need bench tuning + ground-tied throttle tests > before a real airframe goes near them.

peripheral-io.name: etl-fixed-containers
peripheral-io.summary: Builds an `etl::vector<int, 8>` and an `etl::map<const char *, int, 4>` -- two of the Embedded Template Library's fixed-capacity, STL-shaped containers -- and demonstrates their headline property for firmware: **no heap allocation, ever**. Storage for both containers is embedded inline at compile time, sized by the `MAX_SIZE` template parameter.

peripheral-io.name: fmt-formatting
peripheral-io.summary: Formats an `int` + a `float` + a `const char *` into a fixed `char[64]` stack buffer using `fmt::format_to` -- {fmt} (https://github.com/fmtlib/fmt)'s API for writing formatted text through a caller-supplied output iterator instead of returning an owning `std::string`. No heap allocation, no `<iostream>`.

peripheral-io.name: gpio-button-led
peripheral-io.summary: Read a button and toggle an LED -- both as plain GPIO, through the `<alp/blocks/button_led.h>` block helper.

peripheral-io.name: hello-world
peripheral-io.summary: The canonical "first program" for the Alp SDK. No peripherals, no chips, no board-specific wiring -- just a periodic `printf` loop so you can confirm the toolchain, flash flow, and log console are wired correctly before chasing harder bugs.

peripheral-io.name: i2c-device-hub
peripheral-io.summary: Functional read-out of **every populated device on the EVK sensor/power I2C bus**, each through its real chip driver -- not a raw register poke. This is the "prove the whole board is usable" demo: one bus, many ICs, each brought up and read for a real value.

peripheral-io.name: i2c-master
peripheral-io.summary: Discrete I2C master that reads a known device at a known address. Pattern: open the bus, init the chip driver, loop reading the sensor every second, close cleanly.

peripheral-io.name: i2c-scanner
peripheral-io.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/peripheral.h>` I²C. Walks every 7-bit address on `ALP_E1M_I2C0` and reports which respond.

peripheral-io.name: i2c-slave
peripheral-io.summary: Make this MCU answer on the bus as a register-mapped I2C target (slave) using the register-file helper from `<alp/i2c_regfile.h>` (v0.9, `[ABI-EXPERIMENTAL]`), a pure layer over the portable `alp_i2c_target_*` surface in `<alp/peripheral.h>`.

peripheral-io.name: pwm-led-fade
peripheral-io.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/pwm.h>`. Fades an LED on PWM channel `ALP_E1M_PWM0` from 0 % to 100 % and back, demonstrating the canonical open / set-duty / close pattern.

peripheral-io.name: qenc-readout
peripheral-io.summary: Per-peripheral example for the quadrature-decoder side of `<alp/counter.h>`. Reads `ALP_E1M_ENC0`'s position once per 100 ms for ~1 second.

peripheral-io.name: spi-loopback
peripheral-io.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/peripheral.h>` SPI. Demonstrates the canonical transceive pattern.

peripheral-io.name: spi-master
peripheral-io.summary: Discrete SPI master. Send a known byte pattern out MOSI, log whatever clocks back on MISO.

peripheral-io.name: spi-slave
peripheral-io.summary: Claim the bus in target (slave) mode using the portable `alp_spi_target_*` surface from `<alp/peripheral.h>` (v0.9, `[ABI-EXPERIMENTAL]`) and answer an external SPI controller.

peripheral-io.name: uart-echo
peripheral-io.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/peripheral.h>` UART. Reads single bytes from the UART and writes them back.

peripheral-io.name: uart-hello-world
peripheral-io.summary: The canonical "printf via UART" walkthrough. Opens a UART port with `alp_uart_open()`, sends a greeting + a monotonically- increasing counter every second, demonstrates error handling.

peripheral-io.name: uart-rx-ringbuf
peripheral-io.summary: Per-feature example for `<alp/peripheral.h>`'s opt-in interrupt-driven RX ring buffer (`CONFIG_ALP_SDK_UART_RX_RINGBUF`).

peripheral-io.name: usb-host-storage
peripheral-io.summary: Opens the USB host role via `<alp/usb.h>` and enumerates an attached USB mass-storage device on the E1M-AEN401 Cortex-M55-HP.

peripheral-io.name: vendor-ext-composability
peripheral-io.summary: One pad, three ways -- a proof that the Alp SDK's vendor-extension layer (`<alp/ext/<vendor>/...>`) is **additive, never exclusive**.

power-timing.name: counter-alarm
power-timing.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/counter.h>`. Schedules a one-shot alarm 100 ms in the future and prints from the callback.

power-timing.name: littlefs-keyvalue
power-timing.summary: Mounts a littlefs partition, writes a single key/value pair to a file, reads it back, lists the mount-point directory, and asserts the read matches the write. Teaches the Zephyr `fs` subsystem's `fs_mount`/`fs_open`/`fs_write`/`fs_read`/`fs_readdir` API surface using the littlefs backend (`libraries: [littlefs]` in `board.yaml`).

power-timing.name: power-managed-sensor
power-timing.summary: Low-power BME280 + IMU sensor node on the AEN301 M55-HE (high-efficiency) core. The SDK's reference for the v0.6 `cores.<id>.power:` declarative block: a worked example of sleep-mode selection + multi-source wakeup.

power-timing.name: rtc-clock
power-timing.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/rtc.h>`. Sets the wall-clock to a fixed instant, sleeps a few hundred ms, then reads it back to verify the RTC ticked.

power-timing.name: timer-periodic-interrupt
power-timing.summary: The canonical "periodic ISR plus main-thread coordination" example.

power-timing.name: wdt-feed
power-timing.summary: Per-peripheral example for `<alp/wdt.h>`. Installs a 5 second watchdog timeout and feeds it from a background loop.

testing.name: catch2-selftest
testing.summary: A minimal [Catch2](https://github.com/catchorg/Catch2) v3 unit-test binary, built from Catch2's own "amalgamated" single-TU distribution: 3 `TEST_CASE`s over a trivial `clamp()` helper.

testing.name: doctest-selftest
testing.summary: A minimal [doctest](https://github.com/doctest/doctest) v2.4.11 unit-test binary: 3 `TEST_CASE`s over a trivial `clamp()` helper.

v2n.name: v2n-board-id-readout
v2n.summary: Reads + prints the SoM's hardware-info manifest from the on-module 24C128 EEPROM, then asserts the firmware build is intended for the SKU on the module.

v2n.name: v2n-brd-i2c-bringup
v2n.summary: Patch-day diagnostic for the V2N SoM's BRD_I2C management bus (Renesas RIIC8). Scans the bus, distinguishes a bus-level electrical fault (line held low / missing pull-ups / wrong pinmux) from per-device failures, then probes every populated IC read-only and prints a PASS/FAIL/SKIP table.

v2n.name: v2n-eeprom-manifest-dump
v2n.summary: Hexdumps the 128-byte hardware-info manifest at offset 0x0000 of the on-module 24C128 EEPROM, decodes every field, and verifies the CRC. Useful during bring-up to confirm the production-test fixture programmed the module correctly.

v2n.name: v2n-emmc-block-stat
v2n.summary: Query the V2N module's on-module eMMC via Zephyr's disk-access (block-storage) subsystem. Reports sector count + sector size, reads the first 16 blocks (8 KiB) of the device, looks for the MBR signature, and asks the driver for any erase-block / wear-level data it caches.

v2n.name: v2n-ethernet-dual
v2n.summary: Brings up both RTL8211FDI Ethernet PHYs on the V2N module (ET0 + ET1), exercises the management surface (MDIO probe, soft-reset, autoneg restart, link-status read), and configures Wake-on-LAN.

v2n.name: v2n-gd32-bridge-functional
v2n.summary: Single-pass **functional** validation of the V2N module's GD32G553 supervisor-MCU bridge, followed by a forever PWM7 duty staircase as a live oscilloscope observable.

v2n.name: v2n-gd32-bridge-hil-soak
v2n.summary: Pass/fail soak of the **whole GD32 bridge command set** over the 25 MHz SPI fast path. Where [`v2n-gd32-bridge-ping`](../v2n-gd32-bridge-ping) proves the link, this proves the opcodes: every command the bridge firmware implements round-trips each cycle with a self-contained verification — readback compares (PWM, DAC), range sanity (ADC, reset-reason), monotonicity (counter), entropy (TRNG), exact math (TMU sqrt/sin), and documented-sentinel asserts (DA9292 `0xFF`, OTA `NOSUPPORT` on the unarmed image). No external instruments needed; the instrument-grade checks (scope on PWM, precision source on ADC) remain their own HIL-PLAN rows.

v2n.name: v2n-gd32-bridge-loopback
v2n.summary: <!-- Copyright 2026 Alp Lab AB SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 -->

v2n.name: v2n-gd32-bridge-ping
v2n.summary: Continuous PING + `GET_VERSION` liveness probe against the V2N module's on-module GD32G553 supervisor MCU over the 25 MHz SPI fast path. (BRD_I2C is deliberately not opened -- the management-path transport has its own HIL rows.)

v2n.name: v2n-gd32-swd-flash
v2n.summary: Demonstrate the host-driven SWD bit-bang controller ([`chips/gd32_swd/`](../../../chips/gd32_swd/)) by attaching from the Renesas RZ/V2N to the on-module GD32G553 over three GPIOs (SWDIO + SWCLK + optional NRST), reading the SW-DP IDCODE, halting the Cortex-M33, erasing a flash sector, writing + verifying a scratch pattern, and resetting.

v2n.name: v2n-m1-deepx-inference
v2n.summary: DEEPX DX-M1 NPU bring-up + single inference through `<alp/inference.h>` on a V2N-M1 SoM.

v2n.name: v2n-m1-ros-perception
v2n.summary: > ⚠️ **`[UNTESTED]` -- v0.5 paper-correct.** Builds against a > Yocto SDK that includes ROS 2 + the alp-sdk runtime. Real > bring-up (V2N-M1 + DEEPX runtime + camera capture pipeline) > gates on v0.8 V2M HiL.

v2n.name: v2n-power-monitor
v2n.summary: Live per-rail power table from the **E1M-X EVK**'s on-board INA236 current/voltage monitors, read from a Linux/Yocto user-space app on the V2N Cortex-A55.

v2n.name: v2n-pwm-fan-control
v2n.summary: Drive a fan from `ALP_E1M_PWM0` on V2N. Walks duty cycle through a five-stop curve at a fixed 25 kHz board (above audible) using the portable `<alp/pwm.h>` surface, so the wave shape is observable on a scope.

v2n.name: v2n-rtc-multi-alarm
v2n.summary: Exercises the RV-3028-C7's **multi-source event dispatcher**: the RTC has seven distinct interrupt sources (PORF, EXT_EVENT, ALARM, COUNTDOWN, PERIODIC, BSF, CLKF) that all latch into a single STATUS register and route to one physical INT pin. The driver fans the single IRQ out into per-source callbacks so the application's state-machine cleanly separates concerns.

v2n.name: v2n-secure-element-sign
v2n.summary: Probe the OPTIGA Trust M on V2N's BRD_I2C and confirm the current probe-only driver contract. The driver reads I2C_STATE to prove the part is reachable; product-info and raw-APDU helpers return `ALP_ERR_NOSUPPORT` until the Infineon host-library transport is integrated.

v2n.name: v2n-temp-sensor
v2n.summary: Reads the on-module TMP112 once per second and prints the temperature in degrees Celsius (with 0.001 °C resolution). Classic V2N starter app -- one I²C bus open, one chip init, a ten-sample loop.

v2n.name: v2n-xspi-flash-readwrite
v2n.summary: Erase one sector on the V2N module's on-module xSPI NOR, write a known pattern, read it back, and report the comparison. Exercises Zephyr's standard `flash` driver class against the board-facing xSPI bus.

gates.purpose: CI gate: chip-header `@par Driver status:` labels match metadata truth.

Chip driver <-> manifest parity gate -- fails (exit 1) when a chip driver ships without its machine-readable metadata manifest, or a manifest ships with no driver, unless the mismatch is on an explicit allowlist.

Plain-CMake chip-list parity gate -- fails (exit 1) when the root `CMakeLists.txt` `ALP_SDK_CHIP_LIST` (the plain-CMake / Yocto `libalp_chips.a` archive, gated by `ALP_BUILD_CHIPS_ONLY`) drifts from the drivers that actually exist under `chips/<id>/<id>.c`.
Lint repo content for Linux-only idioms in customer-facing surfaces.

Doc-drift gate -- fails (exit 1) when customer-facing documentation references SDK identifiers that no longer exist, when a top-level doc isn't linked from the docs index, or when CC3501E docs/examples describe the current bridge as the obsolete CS-less design.
Doxygen-coverage audit -- walks include/alp/*.h and flags every public function declaration that lacks a `@brief` (or `\brief`) tag in the preceding doc-comment block.
Validate the vendored e1m-spec pinout snapshot and cross-check it against the per-SoM pin-mux tables -- fully **offline** (no network).
E1M board-route <-> SoM capability-table end-to-end gate.
Byte-for-byte `--emit` snapshot regression gate.
Cross-family portability lint for examples/*/{board.yaml,testcase.yaml}.
Reject examples that claim Alp storage coverage while using Zephyr storage APIs.
Inference backend/format <-> dispatcher canonicalisation gate.
CI gate: no hard-coded home-directory paths in executable / build files.
Validate that every silicon pad referenced in the SoM peripheral maps is claimed by **exactly one** peripheral.
Plain-CMake link-completeness gate (issue #593).
CI gate: root public headers stay chip-neutral.
Public/private classifier gate for the public alp-sdk repository.
Validate SoM-release bundle manifests against the public metadata/schemas/som-release-bundle-v1.schema.json contract.
CI gate: every src/backends/**/*_stub.c file must reference an open GitHub issue in its top comment block, on a line of the form:
CI gate: every src/backends/**/sw_fallback.c carries both @par Cost: and @par Performance: tags near the top of the file.
Validate a system manifest against the public IDE/tool contract (metadata/schemas/system-manifest-v1.schema.json).
Test-coverage audit: walk every public header in include/alp/, list the declared functions, and check that at least one test file under tests/ mentions each one.
CI gate: every function declaration under include/alp/ext/**/*.h whose name matches alp_<vendor>_<class>_<verb> must carry an @par Supported silicon: tag in its immediately-preceding Doxygen block.
Fail if any version copy drifts from the declared SDK version.
